name: Barri Booth
description_short: Barri offers check cashing, money transfers, bill payment, and a Visa debit card with 30+ years in remittance services, primarily serving Spanish-speaking customers.
description_long:
Barri is an established money services company with over 30 years of experience in international money transfers and financial services. The company positions itself as a comprehensive financial solution for families sending and receiving remittances, particularly between the United States and Latin America (Mexico, Central America, and South America). They operate both physical store locations and a mobile app platform to serve customers across multiple channels.

Barri's core service offerings include check cashing with competitive rates and extended hours, international money transfers available online, through their app, or at physical locations, bill payment services, mobile phone recharges across the US and Latin America, and a Visa debit card for account management. Additionally, they have partnered with Oportun to offer personal loans ranging from $300 to $10,000. Their mobile application consolidates multiple functions—money transfers, bill payments, account management, and phone recharges—into a single platform available on iOS and Android.

The company distinguishes itself through its extended store hours for check cashing, a user-friendly mobile application rated 4.9 stars, and a long operational history in the remittance industry. Customer reviews highlight ease of use, reliable service, and responsive customer support. The app-based approach allows customers to manage transactions remotely without visiting physical locations, and the partnership with Oportun provides credit-building opportunities through personal loans.

Barri's primary strength is its focus on the underbanked Latino community with multilingual support and a service structure designed for remittance flows. However, the company does not offer traditional banking services like FDIC-insured deposit accounts, limiting it to transactional and money movement services. Fees for check cashing and transfers are mentioned as "convenient" but specific rates are not disclosed on the website, requiring customers to visit locations or contact the company directly.
pros: Check cashing with extended hours and described as fast service with convenient tariffs
Mobile app rated 4.9 stars for money transfers, bill payment, and account management
International money transfer capability to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Partnership with Oportun providing personal loans from $300–$10,000 to build credit history
Multi-channel access: online transfers, mobile app, and physical store locations
Mobile phone recharges available instantly across US and Latin America
30+ years of operational experience in money transfer industry
Bill payment integration allowing management of multiple services through one platform
cons: Specific check cashing fees and transfer rates not disclosed on website—customers must visit location or contact company
No traditional banking services (checking accounts, savings, FDIC insurance) despite positioning as comprehensive financial tool
Limited English-language information; website primarily in Spanish, may exclude non-Spanish speakers
Personal loan partnership is through third party (Oportun), not direct lending, limiting control over terms
Physical store network size and coverage map not detailed on website
best_for: Spanish-speaking immigrants sending remittances to family in Latin America
Unbanked or underbanked consumers needing check cashing and bill payment without traditional bank accounts
Mobile-first users who prefer app-based money management and transfers
Borrowers seeking small personal loans ($300–$10,000) to establish credit history
diagnosis: Barri is best for Spanish-speaking immigrants and underbanked consumers who need check cashing, international remittances, and bill payment services without a traditional bank account. The main caveat is that it is not a bank and does not offer deposit accounts, savings products, or FDIC insurance—it is purely a transaction and money movement service with a lending partnership.
services: International money transfers (online, app, or in-store)
Visa debit card issuance and management
Mobile phone recharges (US and Latin America)
Mobile app for transfers, bill payments, and account management
Physical store locations for in-person transactions
Money transfer to Mexico, Central America, and South America

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 2175 Morse Rd, Columbus, OH 43229-5800, USA
